candidates.title: Potentialities of Nonsmooth Optimization
candidates.summary: In this paper, we show that higher-order optimality conditions can be obtain for arbitrary nonsmooth function. candidates.title: Higher-order optimality conditions with an arbitrary non-differentiable function
candidates.summary: In this paper, we introduce a new higher-order directional derivative and higher-order subdifferential of Hadamard type of a given proper extended real function. This derivative is harmonized with the classical higher-order Fréchet directional derivative in the sense that both de...

candidates.title: Equilibrium stresses and rigidity for infinite tensegrities and frameworks
candidates.summary: Asymptotic equilibrium stresses are defined for countably infinite tensegrities and generalisations of the Roth-Whiteley characterisation of first-order rigidity are obtained. Generalisations of prestress stability and second order rigidity are given for countably infinite bar-jo...

candidates.title: When Chaos Meets Computers
candidates.summary: This paper focuses on an interesting phenomenon when chaos meets computers. It is found that digital computers are absolutely incapable of showing true long-time dynamics of some chaotic systems, including the tent map, the Bernoulli shift map and their analogues, even in a high-...

candidates.title: Second-Order Optimality Conditions in Cone-Constrained Vector Optimization with Arbitrary Nondifferentiable Functions
candidates.summary: In this paper, we introduce a new second-order directional derivative and a second-order subdifferential of Hadamard type for an arbitrary nondifferentiable function. We derive several second-order optimality conditions for a local and a global minimum and an isolated local minim...

candidates.title: Wanted: Floating-Point Add Round-off Error instruction
candidates.authors: Marat Dukhan, Richard Vuduc, Jason Riedy
candidates.summary: We propose a new instruction (FPADDRE) that computes the round-off error in floating-point addition. We explain how this instruction benefits high-precision arithmetic operations in applications where double precision is not sufficient. Performance estimates on Intel Haswell, Int...

candidates.title: Beyond the Lovasz Local Lemma: Point to Set Correlations and Their Algorithmic Applications
candidates.authors: Dimitris Achlioptas, Fotis Iliopoulos, Alistair Sinclair
candidates.summary: Following the groundbreaking algorithm of Moser and Tardos for the Lovasz Local Lemma (LLL), there has been a plethora of results analyzing local search algorithms for various constraint satisfaction problems. candidates.title: The repulsive lattice gas, the independent-set polynomial, and the Lovász local lemma
candidates.authors: Alexander D. Scott, Alan D. Sokal
candidates.summary: We elucidate the close connection between the repulsive lattice gas in equilibrium statistical mechanics and the Lovasz local lemma in probabilistic combinatorics. candidates.title: Kolmogorov complexity, Lovasz local lemma and critical exponents
candidates.summary: D. Krieger and J. Shallit have proved that every real number greater than 1 is a critical exponent of some sequence. candidates.title: Infinite computable version of Lovasz Local Lemma
candidates.summary: Lovász Local Lemma (LLL) is a probabilistic tool that allows us to prove the existence of combinatorial objects in the cases when standard probabilistic argument does not work (there are many partly independent conditions). candidates.title: A new asymptotic enumeration technique: the Lovasz Local Lemma
candidates.summary: Our previous paper applied a lopsided version of the Lovász Local Lemma that allows negative dependency graphs to the space of random injections from an $m$-element set to an $n$-element set.
